title: Primary amines
summary: 9701 AS primary amine chemistry — structure, basicity, preparation, and reactions with PhET molecule builder.
learningObjectives: Distinguish primary, secondary, and tertiary amines.
Explain basicity of amines compared with ammonia.
Describe preparation by reduction of nitriles or amides.
Predict reactions with acids, acyl chlorides, and nitrous acid.

simpleExplanation.title: R–NH₂ nucleophiles
simpleExplanation.summary: Primary amines have one alkyl group on nitrogen. The lone pair makes them basic and nucleophilic — stronger bases than ammonia when alkyl groups donate electron density.
steps: Primary: one R; secondary: two R; tertiary: three R on N.
R–NH₂ + H⁺ → R–NH₃⁺ — alkyl groups increase basicity (+I effect).

sections.content: **Primary amines** (19.1) appear in **organic synthesis** routes and **identification** questions. Contrast **1° vs 2°** products with nitrous acid.
